content.text:
Everything around you is made of a material. Materials are the stuff things are made from — wood, metal, plastic, glass, rubber, and fabric are all materials.

Each material has special properties — properties are words we use to describe what something is like:

🪨 Hard or Soft — A rock is hard. A pillow is soft.
🔊 Rough or Smooth — Sandpaper is rough. Ice is smooth.
💧 Waterproof or Absorbent — A raincoat is waterproof (water slides off). A sponge is absorbent (it soaks up water).
🔍 Transparent or Opaque — Glass is transparent (you can see through it). A wooden door is opaque (you cannot see through it).
⚡ Conductor or Insulator — Metal conducts electricity (lets it flow through). Rubber insulates (blocks electricity).

Scientists choose materials based on their properties. A cup is made of glass or plastic because it is waterproof. A sweater is made of wool because it is soft and warm.

Knowing about material properties helps us build better things and stay safe!

diagrams.alt: Chart showing different materials and their properties

experiment.instructions: Test which material is waterproof! Drag each material into the water bowl and see what happens.

combinations.resultLabel: The raincoat repels water — it's waterproof! The sponge soaks it all up — it's absorbent! Different materials have very different properties.

practiceQuestions.prompt: Which word describes a material that water slides off of?
practiceQuestions.hint: Think about a raincoat — does water soak in or slide off?

practiceQuestions.prompt: Which material would be best for making a window?
practiceQuestions.hint: A window needs to let light through — which material is transparent?
Think about what a sponge or paper towel does to a spill.

questions.prompt: A material that you can see through is called:
Which material would be WORST for making an umbrella?
What property describes whether something feels rough or smooth?
Why are electrical wires covered in rubber?

options.text: Because rubber insulates and blocks electricity

questions.prompt: Which of these correctly describes a rock?
